file_insights.purpose: Database seeding script for E-Menum platform
key_sections: Plans configuration (5 tiers: free, starter, professional, business, enterprise)

patterns_discovered.description: Five-tier pricing structure (free, starter, professional, business, enterprise) with escalating features and limits
Two-scope role hierarchy (PLATFORM and ORGANIZATION) with permission grants via string identifiers
Plans include feature arrays to enable/disable capabilities at different tier levels
Permissions defined as tuples of (resource, action, scope, description)
Each plan includes numeric limits on menus, products, users, storage, and API requests (-1 = unlimited)

gotchas_discovered.description: Enterprise plan uses -1 to represent unlimited resources, requires special handling in enforcement logic
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Ensure comparison logic treats -1 as unlimited throughout codebase

gotchas_discovered.description: Permissions referenced as 'resource.action' strings but defined as separate object properties
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Maintain consistent naming convention between definition and assignment

gotchas_discovered.description: PLATFORM and ORGANIZATION scopes are separate hierarchies; mixing them could cause authorization failures
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Enforce scope validation during role assignment and permission checks

gotchas_discovered.description: All plans hardcoded to TRY currency; internationalization will require schema changes
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Plan for multi-currency support if expanding internationally

gotchas_discovered.description: Diff shows truncation at allergen section (24640 chars total); verify complete allergen list was included
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Review full seed file to confirm all 14 EU allergens present

approach_outcome.description: Successfully created comprehensive database seed file with all required default data
notes: Seed file created in TypeScript with proper Prisma imports
All 5 subscription tiers defined with features and limits
68 permissions defined across 17 resources with organization and platform scopes
9 roles created (4 platform, 5 organization) with permission assignments
Used TypeScript interfaces (PermissionDef, RoleDef) for type safety
Organized into clear sections with separator comments
Follows business rules from BUSINESS_RULES.md and CLAUDE.md
Allergen data section appears truncated in diff; full content needs verification

recommendations.recommendation: Verify complete allergen list is present - diff appears truncated at 24640 chars
recommendations.rationale: Critical for menu compliance; EU requires all 14 allergens

recommendations.recommendation: Create tests for seed script to verify data integrity and relationships
recommendations.rationale: Prevent silent failures in seed execution; ensure relational constraints met

recommendations.recommendation: Implement permission enforcement logic that correctly handles -1 (unlimited) values
recommendations.rationale: Enterprise tier relies on this; incorrect logic will break tier differentiation

recommendations.recommendation: Add comments explaining permission string naming convention (resource.action format)
recommendations.rationale: Developers may struggle with permission assignment syntax

recommendations.recommendation: Consider creating utility functions for common permission queries (e.g., 'does role have permission')
recommendations.rationale: Prevents permission checking logic duplication throughout codebase

recommendations.recommendation: Plan for multi-currency support and dynamic pricing configuration
recommendations.rationale: Hardcoded TRY currency and prices will need adjustment for expansion

recommendations.recommendation: Extract hardcoded values (feature names, permission resources) into constants file
recommendations.rationale: Improves maintainability and prevents typos across codebase
